narrative:
After so many weeks of cold, wintry weather, the warmth of the next seven days will feel almost foreign. A White Legs Warning is in effect today. High pressure is anchored in the Atlantic in a summer-like Bermuda High pattern. High temperatures run just under daily records through Friday. A backdoor front approaches from the northeast this weekend, adding clouds and knocking down temperatures 3–5 degrees. With that front to our northeast and a moist southerly flow, the potential for an afternoon shower creeps up Friday and Saturday.

Unseasonably Warm

Last Updated Thu March 05 at 5:57PM by Ray Russell

After so many weeks of cold, wintry weather, the warmth of the next seven days will feel almost foreign. A White Legs Warning is in effect today. High pressure is anchored in the Atlantic in a summer-like Bermuda High pattern. High temperatures run just under daily records through Friday. A backdoor front approaches from the northeast this weekend, adding clouds and knocking down temperatures 3–5 degrees. With that front to our northeast and a moist southerly flow, the potential for an afternoon shower creeps up Friday and Saturday.

Forecast Discussion

High pressure is centered near Bermuda, putting us in a warm southerly flow for the next seven days. A backdoor front approaches from the northeast this weekend, putting only a slight dent in the warmth.

Today is near-perfect weather with temperatures just below records for the date. Friday is almost as nice and even a degree or two warmer—just watch for the potential for a renegade afternoon shower.

We have more clouds this weekend, and isolated afternoon and evening showers are possible. With the increased clouds, temperatures will be a couple degrees less warm. Sunday starts with clouds, with temperatures slightly less warm than Saturday. I cannot rule out a stray shower Sunday.

Monday and Tuesday, the Bermuda High reasserts itself. We reclaim the warmer temperatures but keep the ever-so-slight chance of an afternoon shower.

A cold front moves through from the west Wednesday night. Wednesday remains warm with more clouds. I cannot rule out a stray shower Wednesday, but a period of rain is expected Wednesday night ahead of the front.

We get a brief cool-down behind the front. Don't think winter is over… A cold outbreak is expected somewhere around St. Patrick’s Day.
